claim: Claude Opus/Sonnet 4.6 with 1M token context maintains useful semantic retrieval past 256k tokens, where competitors (Gemini 3, Llama 4 Maverick) degrade significantly; this is the critical difference in practical long-context utility, not just raw window size.
actionable: When evaluating long-context models, test retrieval quality at 50%, 75%, and 100% of context window; measure semantic degradation curves, not just max window size; Claude Opus 4.6 is reliable past 256k if your use case tolerates some degradation.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Anthropic's decision to offer 1M context at flat pricing (no long-context premium) is a market inflection point; competing labs still charge per-token multipliers at high context, making Claude uniquely cost-effective for context-heavy agentic workflows.
actionable: For long-context agent deployments, use Claude Opus/Sonnet 4.6 at flat pricing; competitors' per-token premiums make them prohibitively expensive; migrate long-context workflows to Claude to reduce inference costs.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Multi-agent orchestration (one agent broadcasting to many specialized agents in parallel) for high-leverage decision-making is underexplored; CEO agents can frame decisions, delegate to expert agents (revenue, technical, moonshot), collect adversarial debates, and synthesize recommendations.
actionable: Design multi-agent systems with a coordinator agent (CEO) that frames problems and collects parallel responses from specialist agents; each specialist has domain-specific system prompts and expertise files; force disagreement to expose blind spots.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Customizable agent harnesses (like Pi) that support front-matter system prompt modifications, dynamic variable injection, and specialized skills are necessary to escape the 'normal distribution' of generic Claude Code or Codex outputs.
actionable: Don't rely on out-of-the-box agent tools; build thin harnesses that parse agent configuration (system prompt, skills, expertise files) from structured front-matter; dynamically inject variables at runtime; specialize every agent.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Agent expertise files (persistent multi-thousand-token memory stores tracking domain patterns, agent relationships, decision history) are more valuable than generic conversational memory; they enable agents to maintain nuanced mental models across multiple decision cycles.
actionable: Implement specialized expertise files for agents (not generic memory); track domain-specific patterns, learned relationships between agent personalities, and decision precedents; use 1M context to store extensive expertise across multiple problem domains.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Constraining agent execution with time budgets and token cost limits (e.g., 5 minutes, $5 per decision) forces concrete decision-making and prevents open-ended deliberation; constraints are critical for production systems.
actionable: Always set hard constraints (time, token budget, cost cap) on multi-agent systems; agents should wrap up and synthesize when constraints hit; this prevents runaway costs and forces decisive outputs.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Specialized agent system prompts with temperament, reasoning patterns, and decision heuristics (e.g., Moonshot agent: 'What if we're thinking too small?', Revenue agent: 'Maximize cash within 90 days') drive distinct personalities that expose mental model gaps when they disagree.
actionable: Design agent system prompts with explicit temperament statements and decision-making heuristics; create agents with opposing philosophies (moonshot vs. revenue, compounder vs. quick-flip); force them to debate so their disagreements surface blind spots.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Briefs (input prompts to agent systems) must follow strict structure templates (situation, stakes, constraints, key questions) to enable agent validation and force human rigor; unstructured prompts produce low-quality decisions.
actionable: Create brief templates with required sections (context, constraints, stakes, decision); validate briefs programmatically before passing to agents; enforce prompt engineering discipline as a system constraint.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Building custom agent harnesses (Pi-style, 2000+ lines) that parse specialized config formats is preferable to prompt-hacking generic Claude Code for critical decision-making; the harness encodes domain logic and ensures consistency.
actionable: For high-stakes applications (business decisions, strategic planning), build thin custom harnesses that enforce configuration structure, validation, and output formatting; don't rely on prompt engineering alone.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Multi-agent adversarial patterns (agents with opposing heuristics debating each other) are more effective for decision-making than seeking consensus; the goal is not agreement but exposure of unresolved tensions and blind spots.
actionable: Design multi-agent systems to surface disagreement, not harmony; explicitly assign agents contradictory objectives (expansion vs. caution, moonshot vs. pragmatic); use tension and unresolved conflicts to signal risk areas.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Out-of-the-box agent tools (Claude Code, Codex generic agents) produce 'normal distribution' outputs unless heavily specialized; competitive advantage requires custom harnesses, specialized system prompts, and domain-specific expertise files.
actionable: Assume generic agent tools are baseline; build thin specialization layers (custom harnesses, prompt patterns, expertise management) to escape commodity performance; focus on system design, not just model selection.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.

claim: Agent knowledge retention across multiple decision cycles (e.g., revenue agent maintaining 11k-token expertise file tracking learned relationships with other agents, decision history, domain patterns) multiplies decision quality more than increasing model size.
actionable: Prioritize building persistent expertise management for agents over switching to larger models; invest in structured memory that agents actually read and update; this compounds with each decision cycle.
source_title: Pi CEO Agents. Claude 1M Context. Multi-Agent Teams.
